rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH A. UREMOVIC VS. UNION BANK, N.A.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Hearing Of Amended Verified Petition Of Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2014, Line 20, PETITIONER ELIZABETH UREMOVIC's Hearing Of Amended Verified Petition Of Writ Of Mandate. Denied. Notwithstanding the procedural defects, the motion is denied on the merits. Petitioner has not attached any evidence showing that she made a request for the release of public records to the appropriate governmental entity prior to filing this petition. (Gov. Code sec. 6259(a).) Petitioner has not established that Respondent is a state or local agency, as defined by Gov. Code sec. 6252. (See also Regents v. University of Cal. v. Sup. Ct. (2013) 222 Cal.App.4th 383.) rulings.Case Title: TRAVELERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA, VS. ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three And Document Demands, Set Three And Req. For Sanctions Against Armada In The Amount Of $4900.00; Appendix And Statement Of Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2014, Line 6, PLAINTIFF TRAVELERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA's Motion To Compel Defendant'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three And Document Demands, Set Three And Request For Sanctions Against Armada In The Amount Of $4900.00. Granted. Within 10 days, Defendant Armada Protective Services, Inc. shall provide further verified responses, without objection, to Travelers' Request for Production Nos. 35, 36, 37, 38, and 39 and Special Interrogatory nos. 64, 65, 66, 67, 68, 69, 70, and 71. These discovery requests are relevant to the subject matter of the pending action. The Court finds that Defendant Armada Protective Services, Inc.'s responses that consist solely of objections are a misuse of discovery. (Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 Cal. App. 3d 771, 783.) Monetary sanctions of $ 4,900.00 are awarded in favor of Travelers Casualty Insurance Company of America and against Defendant Armada Protective Services, Inc. and their counsel, jointly and severally, to be paid within 30 days of service of notice of entry of order. The court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: TRAVELERS CASUALTY INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA, VS. ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Armada Protective Services Inc.'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records; Or In The Alternative, A Protective Order; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT ARMADA PROTECTIVE SERVICES INC.'S Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records; Or In The Alternative, A Protective Order; Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Phil Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. The subpoena was properly served on Samuel Gluck CPA for ArmPro on December 19, 2014 and the records which were requested are relevant. No notice to consumer was required. Any documents which are confidential are to be produced under an agreed upon confidentiality agreement. Any records which are subject to the attorney client or work product privilege must be identified in a privilege log. The subpoena served on Timothy Hyden on January 15, 2014 as the custodian of records for AmPro was also properly served and does not require a notice to consumer. Similarly, the records sought are relevant and are to be produced subject to a confidentially agreement. Any records which are subject to the attorney client or work product privilege must be identified in a privilege log. If Mr. Hyden does not have the records in his position custody or control, that can be stated by proper declaration in response to the subpoena. It appears that either or Vladimir Butenko and Mikhail Butenko, officers or owners of ArmPro (and Armada) are evading service of the subpoena which was eventually served on Mr. Hyden. If Mr. Hyden claims he does not have the ArmPro records he is ordered to state where those records are located. If they are located in the possession of the Butenkos, than service of the subpoena on them may be completed by mailing the subpoenas to them at their last known address and the records requested are ordered to be produced, subject to the conditions stated above regarding confidentially and privileges. The requests for sanctions by both Armada and by Travelers are denied. The responses to the subpoenas to ArmPro and Gluck are due 15 days from the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ABDOL NOVIN, AS TRUSTEE OF THE NOVIN FAMILY TRUST VS. THE TRAVELERS COMPANIES, INC., A CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2014, Line 22, DEFENDANTs TRAVELERS PROPERTY CASUALTY COMPANY OF AMERICA & THE TRAVELERS COMPANIES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The motion for summary adjudication is denied in its entirety. Defendant failed to maintain its initial summary adjudication burden of production. Defendant's evidence shows that there is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant's delay and/or denial in paying benefits was reasonable. For example, Mr. Parrish originally estimated the repairs at approximately $478,000 and defendant has subsequently paid roughly $1.6 million dollars in benefits to date. The trier of fact could find that this great disparity indicates bad faith. In addition, the trier of fact could conclude that defendant's payments were unreasonable in light of the appraisal award. Defendant has not shown that it is "indisputable that the basis for the insurer's denial of benefits was reasonable." Wilson v. 21st Century Ins. Co. (2007) 42 Cal.4th 713, 724. The court denies defendant's motion for summary adjudication as to the claim for punitive damages because defendant failed to maintain its initial burden of production. The Court rejects defendant's argument that an officer, director, or managing agent needed to act maliciously because the separate statement does not raise that issue and that argument was raised for the first time in the reply brief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MCALEESE, PAUL (DBA 530 JACKSON STREET, INC.) VS. RICHARD T. FERKO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Richard Ferko And Richard T. Ferko, A Professional Law Corporation'S Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2014, Line 18, Defendants Richard Ferko And Richard T. Ferko, A Professional Law Corporation's Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint. Defendants' demurrer to Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") is sustained in part and overruled in part. Defendants' demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to individual Plaintiff McAleese. Plaintiff McAleese has failed to allege facts that he is the real party of interest to sue on behalf of corporate Plaintiff 530 Jackson Street. (See Fladeboe v. American Isuzu Motors Inc. (2007) 150 Cal.App.4th 42, 55.) Defendants' demurrer to all causes of action on the grounds that they are barred under the applicable statute of limitations is overruled. Plaintiff alleges that it did not discover the alleged wrongful conduct until May 2012 (SAC 14), and the original complaint was filed on February 14, 2013. The four-year provision under CCP § 340.6(a) is tolled because Defendant continued to represent Plaintiff 530 Jackson Street regarding the underlying litigation. (CCP 340.6(a); SAC 10-19.) Defendants' demurrer to the first and fourth causes of action is overruled as Plaintiff has alleged facts with sufficient particularity to meet the heightened pleading standard of fraud. Defendants' demurrer to the sixth cause of action for accounting is overruled as Plaintiffs have alleged facts that a fiduciary relationship exits and that Plaintiff never received an accounting of the settlement money that Defendants collected. (SAC 10, 13, 36.) =(302/EHG)

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: AMY CHAMBERLIN VS. WHOLE FOODS MARKET CALIFORNIA INC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Attendance At Depo
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2014, Line 5, PLAINTIFF AMY CHAMBERLIN'S Motion To Compel Attendance At Deposition. Pro Tem Judge Phil Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: DENIED. The defendant, Whole Foods Market, (WFM) agreed early on to present a person most knowledgeable and the date which all agreed to was February 27, 2014. In order to preserve its objections, WFM asserted numerous objections to the request for production at the deposition. WFM was not required to produce the documents before the deposition nor was WFM required to provide a list ahead of time identifying which documents it would or would not produce and which documents were privileged. To that extent, the motion is premature. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to philip.andersen.nx3z@statefarm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD YERBIC VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY, A DELAWARE CORPORATION *** Case Transferred to Orange County ***
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2014, Line 19, Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint. Denied. All requests for judicial notice are granted. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") pleads that the defect existed at the time of sale, and thus within the one-year implied warranty duration period prescribed by the Song-Beverly Act. (FAC, 7.) The FAC also pleads that the latent defect could not have been reasonably discovered until August 18, 2011 because Defendant performed "band-aid repairs" to cover up the alleged defect. (FAC, 32, 39, 143-154.) The statute of limitations was tolled until Plaintiff could have reasonably discovered the latent defect, and the complaint is therefore timely. (See Mexia v. Rinker Boat Co. Inc. ((2009) 174 Cal.App.4th 1297, 1304.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD YERBIC VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY, A DELAWARE CORPORATION *** Case Transferred to Orange County ***
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, February 26, 2014, Line 19, Defendant Ford Motor Company'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. Sustained without leave to amend in part and overruled in part. Defendant's requests for judicial notice are granted. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately pleads facts establishing delayed discovery and is thus not barred by the applicable statutes of limitation. Although the FAC alleges that the truck was delivered with "serious defects" (FAC, 7), it also alleges that these defects were not discovered until August 18, 2011 because Defendant performed "band-aid repairs" to cover up the alleged defects. (FAC, 32, 39, 143-154.) As to the first, second, and fourth causes of action, the demurrer is overruled. The FAC alleges intentional tortious conduct independent of a breach of contract. The FAC alleges that Defendants represented to Plaintiff on multiple occasions that they could fix the defects with the engine, even though they knew there was a more serious problem that could only be remedied by replacement. (FAC, 25, 143-154.) Each time Defendant misrepresented that it could repair the truck, which it knew it could not do, it intentionally committed tortious acts independent of the contract. (See Robinson Helicopter Co. v. Dana Corp. (2004) 34 Cal.4th 979, 991; United Guar. Mortg. Indemn. Co v. Countrywide Financial Corp. (C.D. Cal. 2009) 660 F.Supp.2d 1163, 1181.) As to the third cause of action for negligent misrepresentation, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. (See Robinson Helicopter, 34 Cal.4th at 990.) As to the fifth cause of action, the demurrer is overruled. The FAC adequately pleads violation of the Consumer Legal Remedies Act. (FAC, 14-15, 17, 278, 280, 287.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
